61.reason: Unused internal JDK import that causes compilation failure in standard/restricted environments

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  Unused internal JDK import that causes compilation failure in standard/restricted environments
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Unused import that can cause compilation failure in environments running JRE instead of JDK, or with restricted access to internal `com.sun.*` packages]

828.reason: [Incorrect lookahead logic: checking peekSymbol(3) assumes at least 3 tokens ahead exist, which may cause index out of bounds or wrong decisions when fewer tokens are available. Also, VarDecl starts with BType ('int'/'char'), but this condition doesn't properly check for that and incorrectly tries to distinguish Decl from FuncDef by looking at the 3rd token being LPARENT, which is unreliable.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: checking peekSymbol(3) assumes at least 3 tokens ahead exist, which may cause index out of bounds or wrong decisions when fewer tokens are available. Also, VarDecl starts with BType ('int'/'char'), but this condition doesn't properly check for that and incorrectly tries to distinguish Decl from FuncDef by looking at the 3rd token being LPARENT, which is unreliable.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead: checking peekSymbol(3) without ensuring there are at least 3 tokens ahead can cause index out of bounds or wrong decisions; also, the condition for VarDecl detection is flawed since not all non-ConstDecl declarations are necessarily VarDecls just because the 3rd token isn't '(']

836.reason: [Incorrect lookahead logic: checking peekSymbol(2) assumes at least 2 tokens ahead exist. More importantly, FuncDef starts with FuncType ('void'/'int'/'char') followed by Ident, but this condition only checks that the second token isn't MAINTK, which is insufficient to distinguish FuncDef from VarDecl (which also starts with 'int'/'char'). This can cause VarDecls to be misparsed as FuncDefs or vice versa.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: checking peekSymbol(2) assumes at least 2 tokens ahead exist. More importantly, FuncDef starts with FuncType ('void'/'int'/'char') followed by Ident, but this condition only checks that the second token isn't MAINTK, which is insufficient to distinguish FuncDef from VarDecl (which also starts with 'int'/'char'). This can cause VarDecls to be misparsed as FuncDefs or vice versa.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead: checking peekSymbol(2) assumes at least two tokens exist, and the logic is wrong—FuncDef starts with FuncType (void/int/char), but this condition excludes only when the second token is 'main', which doesn't correctly distinguish FuncDef from MainFuncDef or other constructs]
